methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Shinkei, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Saif Khawaja and Reed Ginsberg, has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including firms such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using the $30M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, the analysis yields an implied valuation of $60M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient comparable depth within same-stage and adjacent-stage subsets; the broader peer set lacks the necessary minimum density to support median-based inference.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. This range reflects the single-method result and standard variance around stage-typical progression multiples. Key uncertainties include the lack of narrow peer validation—reliance on a single method leaves the valuation exposed to methodology-specific bias—and the absence of company-specific unit economics or growth metrics that would refine the range. Additional disclosure of ARR, customer concentration, or burn rate would materially strengthen conviction in this valuation corridor.
